title: Add app-owned theme colors
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary

- Adds a package-owned theme palette and exports it for app/icon tooling.
- Renames the chrome `green` theme to `emerald`, keeping green for semantic success states.
- Moves app icon generation to `pollinations.theme` declarations and regenerates React, Enter, Playground, and Model Monitor assets.
- Aligns React app naming to `React UI | pollinations.ai`.

## Checks

- `biome check --write` on touched text files
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/node --test tools/icons/test/*.test.js`
- `jq empty` on touched JSON manifests
- `git diff --check`

gist.headline: Fresh coat of paint
gist.blurb: We've polished up our UI themes and regenerated our app icons for a cleaner, more consistent look across the pollinations ecosystem. The classic green theme is now 'emerald', making way for proper semantic success states.
gist.summary: Added a centralized theme palette, renamed the chrome green theme to emerald, and regenerated app icons across the React UI, Playground, and Enter dashboard.
gist.impact: Users will notice more consistent colors and sharper icons across all pollinations.ai apps, with better visual distinction for success messages.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee as an artist, wearing a painter's beret and holding a palette, painting a glowing app icon. Represents the UI and theme updates.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny painter's beret, holding a wooden palette and paintbrush, painting a glowing emerald green app icon on a wooden easel. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
